title: Carls Funeral

jokes.text: You broke into his cabin and had sex while he and his wife were in the next room.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to help Johnny remember Bob by describing their history
jokes.punchline: The 'helpful' memory is an extremely inappropriate sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff - starts as innocent memory aid, becomes mortifyingly specific TMI. Perfect character beat for Roland's social obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Remember when Bob and I went to that conference in Atlantic City? We shared a hotel room, and one night after a few drinks, things got a little... heated.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I don't think—

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no, let me finish. We ended up in the hot tub together, and Bob said something so funny I nearly drowned laughing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What? It's a beautiful memory!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not helpful, Roland. That's not helpful at all.

jokes.text: Oh... That's Bob.
jokes.setup: Johnny's delayed recognition after the embarrassing sexual story
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment as if this is normal way to remember someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction that shows Johnny trying to maintain dignity while acknowledging the mortifying memory

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Wait, is that how I know you?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: From that story?

jokes.text: No, not Bob. Bob's brother, Carl, died.
jokes.setup: Building dramatic tension about someone dying
jokes.punchline: Classic misdirection - it's not who we think
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection structure but fairly predictable execution

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: We need to talk about what happened. Someone's dead.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? Who?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's your mother.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here, Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know. Your favorite plant died.

jokes.text: And his arms are so weak. Little muscles like corn nuts.
jokes.setup: Johnny making excuses about his back problems
jokes.punchline: Moira's brutally specific and unhelpful addition about his physical weakness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira's attempt to 'help' makes things infinitely worse. 'Corn nuts' is an inspired specific comparison that's both accurate and devastating.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Your back is fine. It's your core that's the problem. You have the abdominal strength of a newborn giraffe.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, we're just talking about my back, sweetheart.
jokes.setup: Moira's unhelpful corn nuts comment
jokes.punchline: Johnny's desperately polite attempt to redirect away from his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny beat - maintaining politeness while internally screaming

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Thank you, Moira. Very helpful. Perhaps we could focus on solving the actual problem at hand rather than discussing snack foods?

jokes.text: Shouldn't bother Carl.
jokes.setup: Discussion about disappointing Bob
jokes.punchline: Darkly noting that the dead man won't care
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - just four words that land the morbid logic

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, he's not going to care. He's dead.

jokes.text: Some townie douchebag is moving in next door. They've got a baby.
jokes.setup: David complaining about new neighbors
jokes.punchline: The immediate irony that these are his own family members
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup - David's snobbery about to backfire spectacularly

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: These new neighbors are absolutely insufferable. Their noise levels are inconsistent with any reasonable standard of—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, those are your family members.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, well, that was my point.

jokes.text: Is there a gun?
jokes.setup: David's disgust at the new neighbors
jokes.punchline: Immediate leap to extreme violence as solution
jokes.explanation: Classic David overreaction - from mild annoyance straight to murder

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not saying we should kill them, but I'm not *not* saying it either.

jokes.text: It's 20 seconds in the microwave not 20 minutes, you tool!
jokes.setup: Couple fighting over thrown hot food
jokes.punchline: Revealing the ridiculous overcooking that caused the fight
jokes.explanation: Establishes the cousins as dysfunctional through domestic incompetence

jokes.text: I've been on enough spring breaks to know how this is gonna end
jokes.setup: David fleeing the situation
jokes.punchline: Comparing family drama to spring break debauchery
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific reference that reveals David's past while commenting on present chaos

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not interested in being part of whatever this is.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Away. Far away. Somewhere without this level of dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's not that bad.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Not that bad? This family makes spring break look like a quiet retreat.

jokes.text: Is anybody in that hollow chest?
jokes.setup: Alexis being abandoned by David
jokes.punchline: Insulting David's physique while demanding his attention
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis beat - shallow insult masking genuine abandonment anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David! David, wait! You can't just leave me here with these people. David, your calves are like... they're like two angry grapes in a tube sock. David!

jokes.text: You know, they say death is just life except you're not here. You're somewhere else, you know? But... But that's okay because at least you're somewhere, you know? But when does... When does somewhere become there? And when does there become here?
jokes.setup: Bob trying to philosophize about death
jokes.punchline: Completely circular nonsensical logic that goes nowhere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Bob's attempt at profundity reveals complete lack of depth. The circular logic is perfectly crafted nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: You know, when you really think about it, death is just... it's the end of life.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: And life is what happens before death. So death is really just the conclusion of what came before it.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Which means life is essentially the beginning of death. Everything we do is just... preparing for the end.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: But the end is only the end because there's a beginning. Without a beginning, there'd be no end.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: So really, death is just life that's finished being life. And life is just death that hasn't started yet.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: So when you think about it... they're kind of the same thing.

jokes.text: For here?
jokes.setup: Bob's existential crisis about location and death
jokes.punchline: Twyla's oblivious coffee service question perfectly punctures the moment
jokes.explanation: Masterful comedic timing - two simple words that perfectly deflate Bob's pretentious rambling. The mundane service question becomes existentially absurd in context.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I think about dying here sometimes. Like, what if I die in this room? What if this is where it all ends?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Would you like some coffee?

jokes.text: Ooh, wow. Ugh. Yikes! That's... That hurts today.
jokes.setup: Johnny continuing to fake back problems
jokes.punchline: Overacted physical comedy of fake pain
jokes.explanation: Functional physical comedy callback but predictable execution

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Ow! My back! Ow, ow, ow! This is excruciating!

jokes.text: C-C-Chris- Carl.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to express condolences
jokes.punchline: Can't even remember the dead man's name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's complete disconnection from the deceased

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I came to express my condolences, but I can't even remember the dead man's name.

jokes.text: And I him. Yeah. And I him.
jokes.setup: Bob saying Carl admired Johnny
jokes.punchline: Johnny's awkward attempt to reciprocate with formal language
jokes.explanation: Nice awkward beat - Johnny's attempt at eloquence becomes stilted and unnatural

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I... I admire Carl as well. He's a very... capable individual. His work ethic is... commendable.

jokes.text: I clam up pret-pretty good.
jokes.setup: Johnny making excuses about public speaking
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's doing exactly what he claims he can't do
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Johnny talking his way into the exact thing he claims he can't do

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not a public speaker. I can't stand up in front of people and just... talk. It makes me incredibly uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Which is why I'm not going to do this keynote address, regardless of how much they're paying me.

jokes.text: They're like really scummy. Like... very skaggy people, who happened to produce a child that hasn't been taken away from them yet.
jokes.setup: Alexis judging the new guests
jokes.punchline: Brutally harsh judgment that will backfire when revealed as family
jokes.explanation: Good setup for dramatic irony payoff - Alexis's snobbery about to meet reality

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, look at this couple. I mean, the outfit choices alone are just... tragic. And did you see how he ordered his coffee? Like, we get it, you're indecisive.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And her shoes? Honey, those went out of style in 2009. Which, granted, was only like five years ago in real time, but still.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait... oh my God. Oh my God, no. That's... that's my cousin and his wife.

jokes.text: The baby's relatively normal looking.
jokes.setup: Alexis's harsh judgment of the family
jokes.punchline: Stevie's hilariously faint praise for the baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - damning with the faintest possible praise

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: The baby is... fine.

jokes.text: They're my cousins.
jokes.setup: Alexis and David's harsh judgment of the guests
jokes.punchline: Casual reveal that these are Stevie's actual family members
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection payoff - completely reframes the entire conversation with perfect deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, did you see how she was dressed? I mean, the hem on that dress was literally touching the ground.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And did you hear her accent? I couldn't understand a word she was saying.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That's my mom.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Bree's not exactly fussy when it comes to introducing men to her vagina.
jokes.setup: Discussion about paternity uncertainty
jokes.punchline: Brutally frank assessment of cousin's sexual habits
jokes.explanation: Peak Stevie - completely matter-of-fact delivery of shocking family information

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, given the number of people your cousin has slept with, I'd say the real question isn't who the father is. It's who it isn't.

jokes.text: I was eating Indian last night, so...
jokes.setup: Ted explaining why he's using Febreze
jokes.punchline: TMI about digestive consequences
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I had some questionable sushi for lunch, and let's just say the Febreze is a necessary precaution for my digestive system's inevitable rebellion.

jokes.text: your worms are almost all gone, aren't they?
jokes.setup: Ted introducing his dogs to Alexis
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of the dogs' parasite issues
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for Ted's oblivious enthusiasm about gross animal facts

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'd like you to meet my dogs. This is Schnaps and this is Ginger.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, they're so cute!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, they're great. Fair warning though, they both have pretty significant parasite issues.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh...

jokes.text: think of them as... our kids.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to make Alexis comfortable with the dogs
jokes.punchline: Escalating to inappropriate parental metaphor way too early in relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Ted moment - his eagerness makes him wildly inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The dogs are actually very friendly. They just need to get to know you. It's like... they're my children, and you're going to be their new mother.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, eventually. When we're ready to take that step in our relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, we've been dating for like three weeks.

jokes.text: I just stood up and sang 'Danny Boy.' Not a dry eye in the house.
jokes.setup: Moira recounting being cornered into giving eulogy
jokes.punchline: Her solution was completely inappropriate but somehow effective
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira story - perfectly reveals her theatrical nature and sets up the episode's climax

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: So there I was, trapped into giving a eulogy for someone I barely knew. What was I supposed to do? I couldn't very well stand up there and admit I had no idea who the deceased was.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: So I did what any reasonable person would do. I gave a ten-minute interpretive dance number set to Enya.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Not a dry eye in the house. They thought it was the most moving tribute they'd ever seen.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, dogs sometimes do that.
jokes.setup: Alexis noting the dog is staring at them in bed
jokes.punchline: Ted's casual acceptance of deeply uncomfortable voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Ted's obliviousness to normal boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Why is the dog staring at us?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Oh, he does that. I think he's just curious.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Curious? Ted, we're in bed together.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's a dog. I don't think he really understands the concept of privacy.

jokes.text: Does... does that mean it's full?
jokes.setup: Big dog sniffing baby's diaper during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Alexis's disgusted realization of what this means
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy - romantic moment destroyed by gross reality

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Is he smelling the... Oh my God, he knows.

jokes.text: Out! Out!
jokes.setup: Dogs increasingly disrupting intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Alexis finally snapping and ejecting the animals
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation payoff but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, can you guys just— can you please just get out?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Get out! Out! OUT!

jokes.text: Well, you know who's sleeping forever, bud? My ex-stepdad, yeah.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for quiet because he wants to sleep
jokes.punchline: Sean's inappropriate death comparison as argument against sleep
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic twist - using death to justify staying awake

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm trying to sleep. Could everyone please be quiet?

dialogue.speaker: Sean
dialogue.line: Sleep? That's just practice for death. Why would you want to practice that?

jokes.text: You smell... very flammable right now.
jokes.setup: David appearing drunk and disheveled
jokes.punchline: Johnny's specific and alarming description of David's alcohol smell
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific word choice - 'flammable' is both accurate and hilariously clinical

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You smell like a brewery had sex with a Christmas tree.

jokes.text: Apparently, he was trying to unhook his belt from the ceiling fan and... apparently it... It got knotted around his neck.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about cause of death
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing embarrassing autoerotic circumstances
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - death circumstances are both tragic and mortifying

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What was the cause of death?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, it was... it was autoerotic asphyxiation.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yeah, he was found... in a very compromising position.

jokes.text: But uh... Carl found the studs on that one.
jokes.setup: Describing Carl hanging from ceiling fan for two days
jokes.punchline: Praising the craftsmanship that enabled the gruesome scene
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - finding the perfect silver lining in a horrific situation. The craftsmanship pride is beautifully absurd and deeply wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, you have to admit, the ceiling fan held up remarkably well.

jokes.text: Mine's shame eating, so yours sounds more fun.
jokes.setup: David saying making out is his drunk habit
jokes.punchline: Stevie's comparison to her own self-destructive coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - reveals Stevie's own issues while maintaining her deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, when I'm drunk, I like to ruin my life, so we all have our thing.

jokes.text: his was wrapped around the remote. Don't worry, I threw out the remote.
jokes.setup: Finding Bree's thong in the room
jokes.punchline: Location reveals what it was used for, requiring disposal of electronics
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with practical consequences - the visual is disgusting but the solution is logical

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Is that... a thong?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It was under the bed.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it didn't get there by itself. And judging by the burn marks, I'd say it was used as a makeshift... what exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: A hair straightener cover.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's disgusting. We're going to have to throw out the entire nightstand.

jokes.text: Nothing says we'll miss you like an excerpt from 'Breaking Dawn,' one of Carl's favourites of the Twilight movies.
jokes.setup: Funeral service beginning
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate choice of vampire romance as tribute to the deceased
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail about Carl's taste and the minister's obliviousness to appropriateness

jokes.text: and now... lying... very, very close to me.
jokes.setup: Johnny's eulogy about Carl being full of life
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable awareness of physical proximity to corpse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Johnny's discomfort with death manifesting in inappropriate honesty

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Carl was full of life. He was vibrant, he was energetic, he was... he was right here.

jokes.text: And yet, for Carl, pretty much an all-consuming thing at this point.
jokes.setup: Calling death a terrible thing
jokes.punchline: Noting that death is now Carl's main characteristic
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic - technically accurate observation that's completely inappropriate for a eulogy

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: Death is a terrible thing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: Now it's Carl's main characteristic.

jokes.text: And-and he loved the feel of big tool in his hand, am I right, Bob?
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to say something nice about Carl
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sexual innuendo at funeral
jokes.explanation: Classic accidental double entendre - predictable but well-executed in context

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Carl was a man who... who knew how to handle things. He had a real knack for getting deep into situations and really... finishing what he started.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: *whispers* Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: *covers face*

jokes.text: when Carl was spinning around on that ceiling fan that he was so very proud of
jokes.setup: Johnny referencing Carl's death circumstances
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate detail about the manner of death at the funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes the death story even more inappropriate by bringing it up at the funeral

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, at the funeral, the priest kept mentioning how Carl died doing what he loved... which was apparently getting stuck in increasingly smaller spaces.

jokes.text: Well... I-I am here to talk about dying in Schitt's Creek! I'm here to talk about the misery of our lives here and how we might never escape this place, and how we do not wanna end up in a coffin, like Carl, here in this... godforsaken-
jokes.setup: Johnny's eulogy going completely off the rails
jokes.punchline: Full meltdown about his own existential crisis at someone else's funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Johnny's complete breakdown becomes about his own fears rather than the deceased. Peak cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm standing here today to say goodbye to someone who was... who was very important. And I keep thinking about mortality, about how we're all just... dust. Aren't we all just dust? I'm dust. You're dust. This podium is dust.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And what's the point? What's any of it for? I spent my entire life building things, acquiring things, and for what? So I can stand here at a funeral and realize it all means nothing?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I don't even know who I am anymore. I came here with nothing. And I'm going to leave with nothing. And somewhere in between, I convinced myself that I was someone important. That I mattered.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And this person we're here to mourn today—they probably felt the same way. They probably lay awake at night wondering if their life had any meaning. And now they're gone. And we're here. And soon we'll be gone too.

jokes.text: ♪ Oh, Danny boy, ♪ ♪ the pipes, the pipes are calling... ♪
jokes.setup: Johnny's eulogy disaster needing to be stopped
jokes.punchline: Moira's exact solution from her earlier story saves the day
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution - Moira's theatrical solution saves Johnny exactly as she described earlier. Beautiful story structure.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I told you about the time I faked my own death at that gala in Monaco!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You simply announce that the deceased has risen, and everyone leaves immediately out of awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It worked then, it'll work now.

jokes.text: And if it's any consolation, the dogs told me to tell you that they're super embarrassed about their behaviour, too.
jokes.setup: Ted apologizing for the dogs' behavior
jokes.punchline: Pretending the dogs expressed shame about their sexual voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Ted's sweet but bizarre attempt to anthropomorphize the dogs

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry about the dogs. They've been acting out since we started them on a new food.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: They're actually very ashamed of what happened. They know what they did was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: We've had several conversations about appropriate boundaries and consent.

jokes.text: There'd be slutty little animals running around.
jokes.setup: Alexis realizing Ted is a vet
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that all animals in vet care are sexually promiscuous
jokes.explanation: Excellent Alexis logic - projecting human sexual behavior onto sick animals. Perfectly absurd character reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, you're a vet? Like a veterinarian?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yes, that's correct.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God. So you see animals... naked?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, animals don't typically wear clothes, so—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's so weird. Don't they feel like, embarrassed? I would be mortified if someone saw me naked like that.

jokes.text: And I only take in the slutty ones, so...
jokes.setup: Alexis's concern about slutty animals
jokes.punchline: Ted playing along with the absurd premise completely seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan commitment to Alexis's absurd premise. Ted's innocent delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm just concerned about all these slutty animals running around.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, you know, that's actually a very legitimate concern. We do see a lot of promiscuous behavior in the animal kingdom.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Perhaps we should consider some sort of animal chastity program.

jokes.text: Is it a credit card?
jokes.setup: Ted giving her something from a book
jokes.punchline: Alexis's materialistic first assumption
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Alexis's priorities and worldview

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I got you something from a book I was reading.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is it a Birkin?

jokes.text: You know where I got my hat, brah? Where? 'Assholes' R 'Us'.
jokes.setup: Sean engaging Johnny in conversation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of being an asshole through fake store name
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat showing Sean's crude self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Sean
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been thinking about opening my own store.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What kind of store?

dialogue.speaker: Sean
dialogue.line: I'm gonna call it 'A**hole Emporium.' You know, so people know what they're getting into right away.

jokes.text: My hat.. I'm wearing it. I'm wearing it...
jokes.setup: David and Sean getting physical over the marijuana
jokes.punchline: Sean's stoned realization about his own hat
jokes.explanation: Functional stoned humor callback but not particularly inspired

dialogue.speaker: Sean
dialogue.line: Wait... this whole time... my hat's been on backwards.

jokes.text: She's not dead, but... She's been coughing a lot lately.
jokes.setup: Bob asking Moira to sing at his cousin's funeral
jokes.punchline: Wanting to book funeral entertainment for someone still alive
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Bob's morbid planning and Moira's effectiveness at funerals

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Would you be interested in singing at my cousin's funeral?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I would be delighted to, but isn't your cousin still alive?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yes, but we like to book entertainment in advance.

jokes.text: So, who's pig do we have to slaughter to get an invite?
jokes.setup: Learning about Hawaiian night with roasted pig
jokes.punchline: Johnny's attempt at a pig-related idiom modification
jokes.explanation: Weak dad joke - Johnny's forced pig pun is predictable and low-effort

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A roasted pig? What am I supposed to do with a roasted pig?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's Hawaiian night, Dad. It's a whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not going to make a pig's ear out of a silk purse.

tmdb_overview: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he doesn’t really know. Alexis struggles with her new boyfriend’s love of animals.
insight: Character comedy dominates an 81-point episode built on funeral-setting cringe and social awkwardness.
